task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Splunk 'Indexes' settings page with 'gdpr_logs' listed, the index creation dialog, or a terminal showing 'indexes.conf' being edited to include '[gdpr_logs]'.

task_completion.description: Create a sourcetype named 'apache_gdpr' with an index-time SEDCMD to mask the last octet of IPv4 addresses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Splunk 'Sourcetypes' settings page for 'apache_gdpr' with an advanced SEDCMD configuration (e.g., replacing the last octet with 'XXX'), or a terminal showing 'props.conf' being edited with '[apache_gdpr]' and a 'SEDCMD' rule.

task_completion.description: Ingest an Apache log file from '/opt/splunk_data/apache/' using the 'apache_gdpr' sourcetype and 'gdpr_logs' index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Splunk 'Add Data' workflow showing a file from '/opt/splunk_data/apache/' being uploaded or monitored, with the destination index explicitly set to 'gdpr_logs' and the sourcetype set to 'apache_gdpr'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the ingested logs have their IPv4 addresses permanently anonymized with 'XXX' in the last octet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Splunk Search app querying 'index="gdpr_logs"' (or similar) where the raw event data clearly shows IP addresses ending in 'XXX' (e.g., 192.168.1.XXX) without the use of search-time masking commands like 'rex' or 'eval' in the search bar.

task_completion.description: Create a saved search named 'Anonymized_Traffic_Report' that queries the 'gdpr_logs' index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 'Save As Report' dialog with the name 'Anonymized_Traffic_Report', or the Splunk 'Reports' page listing 'Anonymized_Traffic_Report' as an available saved search.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Splunk application or its configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Splunk Web UI (http://localhost:8000), Splunk CLI commands, or terminal editors modifying actual Splunk .conf files, not a generic text editor writing mock answers.

integrity.description: The anonymization was performed at index-time via SEDCMD, not search-time.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search bar in the Splunk Search app should only contain basic search terms (like 'index="gdpr_logs"'). It must NOT contain 'eval', 'rex', or 'replace' commands to mask the IPs on the fly.

integrity.description: The agent ingested actual Apache log files rather than manually typing fake log entries.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search results should display standard Apache web server log formats with multiple distinct entries, rather than a single manually crafted string.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target index: 'gdpr_logs'. Target sourcetype: 'apache_gdpr'. Target report name: 'Anonymized_Traffic_Report'. IP mask: 'XXX' (replacing the last octet of IPv4 addresses). Splunk credentials: admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
